title: Falcon-X: a Time Series Foundation Model for Heterogeneous Multivariate Modeling
abstract: We introduce a Falcon foundation model for time series forecasting and heterogeneous multivariate modeling.
notes: Falcon is a model name here, not the lattice signature scheme.

title: Practical Anonymous Two-Party Gradient Boosting Decision Tree
abstract: This work studies privacy-preserving machine learning for anonymous two-party computation and secure gradient boosting decision trees.
notes: Privacy-preserving ML should not become AI4Lattice without lattice or cryptanalysis evidence.

title: A Generic Transformer Foundation Model for Scientific Time Series
abstract: We train a neural foundation model for forecasting and representation learning on scientific time series.
forbidden_sections: BKZ / LLL / G6K / Lattice Reduction / Attacks
notes: Generic AI without lattice/LWE/cryptanalysis anchors stays in watchlist.

title: Fast Private Information Retrieval for Cloud Databases
abstract: A PIR protocol improves client privacy for database queries using communication-efficient secure retrieval.
notes: PIR is routed to general privacy unless lattice/PQC evidence appears.

title: Production Implementation Audit for Distributed Systems
abstract: We audit implementation bugs, software performance, and benchmark regressions in production distributed systems.
notes: Implementation evidence alone should not imply PQC.

title: Transformer for LWE Coordinate Selection
abstract: A neural Transformer is used as an AI-assisted lattice cryptanalysis subroutine for LWE coordinate selection.
notes: Explicit Transformer + LWE + lattice cryptanalysis should trigger AI4Lattice and LWE.

title: BKZ Attack on Sparse LWE with Hybrid Guessing
abstract: The paper studies sparse LWE, BKZ cost estimates, dual attack, primal attack, and hybrid attack strategies.
expected_sections: BKZ / LLL / G6K / Lattice Reduction / Attacks
notes: No AI/ML terms are present, so attack + LWE should not trigger AI4Lattice.

title: ML-DSA Production Implementation Audit
abstract: We audit constant-time behavior, side-channel leakage, and fault handling in Dilithium and ML-DSA implementations.
notes: PQC scheme plus implementation/security evidence should trigger both sections.

title: Module-SIS Chameleon Hash Commitments
abstract: A lattice-based commitment and chameleon hash construction from the Module-SIS assumption.
notes: Module-SIS primitive case should stay in SIS/NTRU/commitment section.

title: Falcon Signature Implementation on Embedded Devices
abstract: Constant-time implementation and side-channel evaluation of the Falcon post-quantum signature scheme.
notes: Falcon with signature and implementation context should trigger PQC and implementation sections.

title: Differentially Private Federated Fine-Tuning with RLWE Secure Aggregation
abstract: This paper studies DP-SGD, LLM fine-tuning and federated learning with RLWE-based secure aggregation and lattice-based homomorphic encryption for ML.
notes: Privacy/FL/LLM terms co-occur with RLWE and lattice HE anchors.

title: Lattice-Based Secure Aggregation for Federated Learning
abstract: A lattice-based homomorphic encryption protocol supports secure aggregation for federated learning.
notes: Lattice secure aggregation for FL is privacy/FL, not AI4Lattice.

title: The Lattice Isomorphism Problem for Post-Quantum Cryptography
abstract: We study lattice isomorphism, lattice automorphism and structured lattice isomorphism as advanced lattice assumptions.
notes: Explicit lattice isomorphism should trigger the advanced assumptions section.

title: LWE-Based Registration-Based Encryption
abstract: A public-key encryption with registration construction uses LWE and registration-based key management.
notes: Registration-based encryption plus LWE is an advanced encryption primitive case.

title: Lattice-Based Anonymous Credentials from Module-SIS Commitments
abstract: The primitive combines lattice-based zero-knowledge proof, anonymous credential, ring signature and Module-SIS commitment.
notes: Advanced primitive with lattice and Module-SIS evidence.

title: Lattice-Based Zero-Knowledge Proofs for Module-SIS Relations
abstract: We construct lattice-based proof systems and zero-knowledge arguments for Module-SIS relations.
notes: Lattice-based ZK with Module-SIS evidence belongs to advanced primitives.

title: DP-SGD for LLM Fine-Tuning
abstract: Differential privacy for federated fine-tuning of large language models using optimizer-level clipping and noise.
notes: Generic DP/LLM work without lattice/PQC/HE anchors is not lattice privacy.

title: Communication-Efficient Federated Learning
abstract: A generic federated learning optimizer for mobile devices without cryptography.
notes: FL alone is not enough.

title: Graph Isomorphism Algorithms for Neural Models
abstract: This paper studies graph isomorphism and model isomorphism in neural networks.
notes: Graph/model isomorphism should not trigger lattice isomorphism.

title: User Account Registration System
abstract: We study account registration workflows, login UX and database account management.
notes: Generic user registration is not RBE.

title: Generic Zero-Knowledge Proofs for Anonymous Credentials
abstract: A generic zero-knowledge proof system for anonymous credentials using standard discrete-log style examples.
notes: Generic ZK/privacy primitive goes to general privacy without lattice/PQC evidence.

title: Homomorphic Feature Matching for Machine Learning
abstract: A homomorphic-sounding representation learning method for ML features and neural embedding alignment.
notes: Homomorphic-sounding ML without cryptographic HE/FHE evidence is not a lattice privacy hit.
